title: Non-destructive DNA extraction from DESS preservation solution
author: Eri Ogiso-Tanaka

steps_raw.instruction: Sample storage using DESS solution (see Materials page) To transport or store the DNA as undamaged as possible, place the specimen or environmental sample in DESS solution. *Cautions when the sample is an animal Since the DESS solution has low toxicity, animals are not immediately killed when placed in the DESS. It is advisable to kill it somehow before putting the animals into the DESS. This treatment is not particularly necessary for microscopic specimens (e.g. meiofauna). Samples in DESS should be stored in the dark at room temperature (10 ~ 35゜C)
Transfer X µL (eg. 500 µL ) of DESS supernatant with the sample to the tube.

Add 1 volume of Binding buffer (eg. 500 µL ) to the tube. Binding Buffer 5M Guanidine Thiocyanate 4% Triton X100 10% Tween 20 10mM Sodium acetate *If it is not possible to prepare the Binding Buffer, AL buffer Qiagen Catalog #19075 can also be used.
Mix by turning upside-down for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Add 10 µL silica solution into new 1.5mL LoBind tube. Silica solution *mix well before aliquote 2.5g wakosil (FUJIFILM, Cat.#232-00841) 11mL 0.01N HCl
9100 x g, 20 °C, 1 minute and discard the supernatant.
Add 500 µL of Wash Buffer 1 and mix by vortexing. Wash Buffer stock buffer : Ethanol = 1: 4 ratio Stock buffer 10mM TrisHCl pH8.0 100 mM NaCl 0.5mM EDTA pH8.0
9100 x g, 20 °C, 1 minute and discard the supernatant with a pipette (or carefully with an aspirator).
Add 500 µL of Wash Buffer 2 and mix by vortexing.
9100 x g, 20 °C, 1 minute and discard the supernatant with a pipette (or carefully with an aspirator).
9100 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute and completely discard the supernatant with a pipette (or carefully with an aspirator).
Air dry for 00:01:00 at Room temperature . *Be careful not to over-dry.
Add 103 µL TE and mix by vortexing.

Incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 . (DNA dissolves in TE. ) *While incubating, prepare the following 1) Prepare a new 1.5mL LoBind tube. 2) Add 10 µL 3M sodium acetate to the tube. 3) Add 1 µL glycogen *optional
9100 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
Transfer the 100 µL supernatant to a new 1.5 mL LoBind tube prepared in step 15.
-20 °C 00:05:00 ~ *Cool the centrifuge at this time.
20400 x g, 4 °C, 00:30:00 and discard the supernatant with a pipette (or carefully with an aspirator). *max speed
Add 1 mL 75% Ethanol to the tube . *Don't mix
Add 1 volume of Ethanol (eg. 500 µL ) to the tube.
Add 100 µL isopropanol to the tube and mix by vortexing.
20400 x g, 4 °C, 00:01:00 and discard the supernatant with a pipette (or carefully with an aspirator). *max speed
9100 x g, °C, 1 minute and completely discard the supernatant with a pipette (or carefully with an aspirator). *A small benchtop centrifuge can be used.
Air dry for 00:10:00 〜 00:30:00 at Room temperature in the dark. *Be careful not to leave any ethanol residue and not to over-dry.
Add 10 µL 0.1 TE to the tube and mix by vortexing.
Incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature in the dark.
PCR using 1 µL supernatant.
